mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLA250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLA250 2.0-litre turbo engine typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £1,800–£3,500. A full engine replacement can cost anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes-Benz engine replacements typically around £3,500 or more. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLA250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you have. The first-generation M270 (2013–2019) has known issues: timing chain tensioner wear (especially with delayed oil changes), a camshaft weld recall on 2015 models, and PCV valve failures. The second-generation M260 (2019–present) is an improvement but still has timing chain and PCV concerns. Regular maintenance using MB 229.5 spec oil is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLA250?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain tensioner wear and stretch (rattling on cold start, misfires, lack of power), camshaft weld failure (2015 model recall — engine damage and stalling risk), PCV valve failure (check engine light P052E, rough idle), and engine mount wear.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes CLA250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and which generation. Against a well-kept £18,000 CLA250, a £4,000 recon is 22% — worth it. Against a tired £6,000 example, the same recon approaches our 70% Mercedes threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLA250 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. C117/X117 (2013–2019): M270 DE20 AL. C118/X118 (2019–present): M260. The chassis code is the easiest tell — C117/X117 is the first-generation CLA, C118/X118 is the second-generation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain on the CLA250, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £800–£2,000. Skipped oil changes are a primary cause of tensioner wear. If ignored and the chain snaps, the engine is an interference design — pistons and valves collide, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil are the single most effective preventative measure.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear & Stretch — M270 & M260
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting a few seconds, metallic ticking from the engine, engine misfires, rough running, lack of power, difficulty starting.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles (some owners recommend servicing at 70,000 miles)
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 (chain + tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£7,500 (if chain snaps and valves strike pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain tensioner wear is a known issue on both M270 and M260 engines. Skipped oil changes are a primary cause — gritty or poor-quality oil starves the tensioner of proper lubrication. The M270 engine is prone to rattling timing chains, especially if oil changes were delayed. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain and tensioners inspected immediately. Regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Camshaft Weld Failure — M270 (2015 Model Year)
problems.symptoms: Engine damage, stalling, engine warning light, loss of power, potential crash risk.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affected specific 2015 CLA250 vehicles
problems.repairCost: Free under recall (camshaft replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check if your vehicle is affected by the recall

problems.recommendation: Mercedes issued a recall for certain 2015 CLA250 vehicles due to intake or exhaust camshafts whose welds may fail and cause engine damage. Engine damage may result in a stall, increasing the risk of a crash. Mercedes-Benz will notify owners and replace the affected camshafts free of charge. If you own a 2015 CLA250, check if your vehicle is affected by this recall immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full camshaft failure guide

problems.title: PCV (Crankcase Ventilation) Valve Failure — M270 & M260
problems.symptoms: Check engine light (code P052E), rough idle, engine running poorly at idle, oil consumption.
problems.repairCost: £200–£600 (PCV valve replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£7,500 (if left untreated leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: Mercedes issued an extended warranty for certain vehicles with a check engine light code P052E. The component that malfunctioned is the crankcase valve (PCV valve), which has caused issues while the car is idling. If you experience rough idle or a check engine light, have the PCV system inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full PCV failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLA250? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2019 M260 preferred over early M270 (improved design)
* Full Mercedes service history with evidence of regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil
* No cold-start rattle — a quiet startup suggests healthy timing chain
avoidIf: * 2015 model with no proof of camshaft recall completion
* Cold-start rattle with no timing chain history
* Check engine light (P052E) with no PCV repair history
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLA250 is Mercedes' stylish entry-level four-door coupe, powered by a 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder. The first-generation M270 engine has known issues: timing chain tensioner wear, a camshaft weld recall on 2015 models, and PCV valve failures. The second-generation M260 is an improvement but still has timing chain and PCV concerns. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained CLA250 is a £15,000–£30,000 premium coupe; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare CLA250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes CLA250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — including which engine era (M270 or M260) and confirmed oil-change intervals using MB 229.5 spec oil
* Camshaft Recall (2015) — Has the camshaft weld recall been completed? (critical for 2015 models)
* PCV Check — Any history of check engine light (P052E) or rough idle? (PCV valve warning)
* Engine Mounts — Any vibration or clunking? (engine mounts are common failures)
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke on startup can indicate turbo or valve seal issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, PCV, or camshaft issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
